# Runbook — Vendor-estimate importer (`estimate_poll`) (an uploaded estimate stuck / refused / dark) (Successor-Remediation, Op Stds §43)

<!-- BEGIN TREE-XREF (generated by scripts/build_runbook_xrefs.py — do not edit inside) -->
> **In the troubleshooting tree** ([printable guide](../troubleshooting/troubleshooting_guide.md) · dashboard `/troubleshoot`). This runbook resolves:
>   - Purchase order — build, config, pull/render/file, send (dark) → estimate-poll screens, classifies, and files an uploaded vendor estimate (dark) → “Uploaded estimates are not being pulled/filed.”
>   - Purchase order — build, config, pull/render/file, send (dark) → estimate-poll screens, classifies, and files an uploaded vendor estimate (dark) → “An uploaded estimate is refused (wrong doc type, SUSPICIOUS/MALICIOUS screen, or an integrity failure).”
> Daemon(s): `estimate-poll` — see the [daemon reference](../references/daemon_reference.md).
<!-- END TREE-XREF -->

A §43 successor-remediation entry, written for the **Successor-Operator**: a trained
operator who runs Claude Code and reads Smartsheet rows + alert emails, but does **not**
read code. Claude loads the relevant block to drive a Tier-2 repair; the operator sees the
ITS_Errors / ITS_Review_Queue / ITS_Daemon_Health / Estimate_Log evidence and approves.
The §42 code-reader rationale lives in the module docstring of
`po_materials/estimate_poll.py`.

`po_materials/estimate_poll.py` is part of the **generation half of the External Send
Gate** (FM v11 Invariant 1) — it is AI-free (cloud **and** local) and customer-send-free.
The office uploads a vendor's estimate PDF through the portal; the Cloudflare Worker
(`safety_portal/worker/po_estimates.ts`) queues it **send-free** in D1 and signs it
(`est:v1`); this launchd daemon (`org.solutionsmith.its.estimate-poll`, every 120 s) is
the Mac-side consumer, behind ONE gate (`po_materials.estimate_poll.polling_enabled`,
shipped `false`). Per document it:

1. **Claims** the row, pulls the bytes, and **verifies** them (est:v1 HMAC + SHA-256
   recompute) — a failure here is an INTEGRITY event (Symptom 4), never processed further.
2. **Screens** the document (§34 — the same screener as PO attachments; the optional
   ClamAV layer reuses `po_materials.po_attach_screen.clamav_enabled`).
3. **Classifies** the document type. **Invoices and A/P reports are REFUSED from the PO
   path by design** — they are never parsed as purchase line items (Symptom 6 — usually
   not a fault).
4. **Files** clean documents to Box (`<job>/Purchase Orders/Vendor Quotes/`) + an
   `Estimate_Log` row, renders **page previews** for the disposition screen, and reports
   `needs_review`. A human enters/accepts every dollar in the portal's disposition
   screen — this daemon extracts nothing and sends nothing.

> **The both-rule (FM v11 / Op Stds §44).** A fault is Tier-2-eligible (the
> Successor-Operator may self-repair) only if it is **documented here AND
> low-capability-class**. Anything touching the **External Send Gate, secrets/auth,
> doctrine, or code** is FIXED high-class → escalate to Seth (the Developer-Operator)
> regardless of documentation. For `estimate_poll` the most common escalations are an
> **HMAC/integrity failure** (security), a **MALICIOUS screening verdict** (security),
> and **missing/rejected credentials** (secrets/auth).

## The one-shot flag file — how PERMANENT refusals are retried

Permanent refusals (integrity failures, screen refusals, wrong-doc-type refusals) are
recorded in `state/estimate_poll_flagged.json` as `{estimate_id: reason}` and **skipped
every subsequent cycle** (no Review-Queue spam every 120 s).

**Retry after fixing the cause = delete the estimate's entry from
`state/estimate_poll_flagged.json`** (or delete the whole file to retry all). The next
cycle re-pulls the row. Appropriate ONLY for the low-class cases below (e.g. a wrongly
classified document the operator wants re-run after a fix) — **never** for
integrity/HMAC or MALICIOUS flags, which escalate.

Note: a screen/doc-type refusal also posts `refused` back to the Worker, which
**deletes the document bytes from D1** — a refused estimate cannot be re-run from the
pool; the office re-uploads it. An integrity-failed row posts nothing; its bytes stay in
D1 for forensics.

---

## Symptom 1 — nothing happens at all (dark / not installed)

### Symptom
No `estimate_poll` rows in ITS_Daemon_Health, no `estimate_cycle_summary` INFO lines,
uploads sit in the portal as "received".

### What it means
Either the gate is off (the shipped default) or the launchd job isn't loaded. This is the
**intentional dark state** until go-live.

### What the Successor-Operator checks / does (low-class)
1. ITS_Config → `po_materials.estimate_poll.polling_enabled` (Workstream
   `po_materials`). `false` = dark by design. **Read the row's full Description before
   flipping** — it names the go-live preconditions; if any is unmet, the flip escalates.
2. If the gate is `true` but no heartbeat: `launchctl list | grep estimate-poll`. If
   absent, the plist was never loaded — loading a daemon accompanies go-live and is done
   with Seth (Symptom-1 of `docs/runbooks/po_poll.md` is the same shape).

### Escalate-to-Seth condition
First-ever activation is a go-live step (gate flip + plist load + smoke) — always with
Seth.

---

## Symptom 2 — fail-closed: missing estimate credentials (`estimate_creds_missing`, CRITICAL)

### Symptom
CRITICAL email/SMS citing `estimate_creds_missing`; ITS_Daemon_Health row ERROR with
"estimate portal credentials missing"; nothing polls.

### What it means
The Worker base URL (ITS_Config `safety_reports.portal.worker_base_url`), the estimate
bearer (Keychain `ITS_PORTAL_ESTIMATE_TOKEN`), or the HMAC secret (Keychain
`ITS_PORTAL_HMAC_SECRET`) is unset/unreadable. The daemon fails CLOSED — it will not
poll until fixed, and this will not self-heal.

### Escalate-to-Seth condition — **secrets/auth = FIXED high-class → Seth**
Keychain entries are secrets. Do not attempt to create/rotate them.

---

## Symptom 3 — estimate bearer UNAUTHORIZED (`estimate_bearer_rejected`, CRITICAL)

### Symptom
CRITICAL citing `estimate_bearer_rejected`; ITS_Daemon_Health ERROR; uploads stay queued.

### What it means
The Worker rejected the bearer (401) — rotated/mismatched `ITS_PORTAL_ESTIMATE_TOKEN`
vs the Worker's `PORTAL_ESTIMATE_API_TOKEN`. Everything stays queued (safe re-attempt
once fixed).

### Escalate-to-Seth condition — **secrets/auth = FIXED high-class → Seth**

---

## Symptom 4 — INTEGRITY FAILURE (`estimate_integrity_failure`, CRITICAL, one-shot-flagged)

### Symptom
CRITICAL citing `estimate_integrity_failure`; a **security-flagged** ITS_Review_Queue row
"estimate: INTEGRITY FAILURE … rejected, NOT screened or filed".

### What it means
The pulled row failed the est:v1 HMAC verify, or the reassembled bytes disagree with the
signed SHA-256/size, or the chunk set was malformed. The document was **never screened,
never parsed, never filed**; its bytes stay in D1 for forensics; no result was posted.
This is either tampering or a serious serving defect — both are security surface.

### Escalate-to-Seth condition — **security = FIXED high-class → Seth (always)**
Never delete this flag entry to "retry" — the refusal IS the defense.

---

## Symptom 5 — a document screened MALICIOUS or SUSPICIOUS (`estimate_malicious` / `estimate_suspicious`)

### Symptom
MALICIOUS: CRITICAL **naming the uploading account**, a security-flagged Review-Queue
row, Estimate_Log `refused` with `Detail` starting `screen:malicious:`.
SUSPICIOUS: a WARN Review-Queue row (security-flagged only for structural active
content), Estimate_Log `refused`, `Detail` starting `screen:suspicious:`.

### What it means
The §34 screener refused the file before filing: macro payloads / zip or decompression
bombs / ClamAV hits are malicious; PDF active-content markers, magic/extension
mismatches, unreadable structure are suspicious. The file never reached Box.

### What the Successor-Operator checks / does
* **SUSPICIOUS, plainly explainable** (e.g. the office saved a quote as `.docx` with an
  odd extension, an over-size scan): low-class — tell the uploader to re-export a plain
  PDF and re-upload. Resolve the Review-Queue row.
* **MALICIOUS — always escalate**: security = FIXED high-class → Seth. Do not ask the
  account to re-upload until Seth has reviewed the account.

---

## Symptom 6 — refused as INVOICE / A/P REPORT (`estimate_wrong_doc_type`, usually NOT a fault)

### Symptom
Estimate_Log row `Status=refused`, `Detail=wrong_doc_type:invoice` (or `:ap_report`);
a POLICY_EDGE WARN Review-Queue row; the portal shows the upload refused.

### What it means
**Working as designed** (ADR-0004): an invoice or A/P report must never enter the PO
path as purchase line items. The classifier is deterministic keyword scoring — a vendor
whose *quote* is billing-worded can occasionally trip it.

### What the Successor-Operator checks / does (low-class)
1. Open the document the office uploaded. If it really is an invoice/AP report — done;
   resolve the Review-Queue row and route the document to its proper (accounts) home.
2. If it is genuinely a quote misclassified: the bytes were deleted on refusal, so the
   fix is **re-upload**. If the same document trips again, that is a classifier-rule
   change = **code → Seth** (who can also advise a workaround, e.g. re-exporting the
   quote pages only).

---

## Symptom 7 — Box portal root unresolved (`estimate_box_root_unresolved`, left claimed, NOT flagged)

### Symptom
ERROR rows citing `estimate_box_root_unresolved` each cycle; the estimate stays claimed
and re-tries.

### What it means
ITS_Config `safety_reports.box.portal_root_folder_id` (Workstream `safety_reports`) is
unset — the shared Box mirror-tree root every portal artifact files under. Nothing is
lost; the row retries every cycle.

### The low-class Tier-2 action
Confirm the row exists and holds the Box folder id the other daemons use (portal_poll /
po_poll file fine when it is right). If the row is present and the error persists →
escalate (Box auth is secrets-adjacent).

---

## Symptom 8 — Estimate_Log writes failing (`estimate_log_write_failed` WARN, or a RuntimeError naming SHEET_ESTIMATE_LOG)

### Symptom
WARNs citing `estimate_log_write_failed`, or per-row errors mentioning
"SHEET_ESTIMATE_LOG is still the 0 placeholder".

### What it means
The Estimate_Log sheet either hasn't been built/flipped yet
(`scripts/migrations/build_estimate_log_sheet.py` prints the id; flipping it in
`shared/sheet_ids.py` is a **code change**) or Smartsheet writes are transiently failing.
Refusal/filing dispositions still proceed — the Review-Queue row + D1 remain the record;
the ledger self-heals on later stamps only for rows it has.

### Escalate-to-Seth condition
The builder-run + id-flip is a **code change = FIXED high-class → Seth**. Transient
Smartsheet failures self-heal; persistent ones follow the general Smartsheet-outage
runbook.

---

## Symptom 9 — no previews for a document (`estimate_preview_empty` / `estimate_preview_failed`, WARN)

### Symptom
WARN citing `estimate_preview_empty` or `estimate_preview_failed`; the disposition
screen shows the document without page images and demands the explicit "no preview"
acknowledgment before any accept.

### What it means
The Quartz render child timed out, crashed, or the PDF is unrenderable (this is the
**designed degrade** — a hostile or broken PDF kills the render CHILD, never the
daemon). The document itself still filed to Box and is reviewable there.

### The low-class Tier-2 action
None required — the no-preview acknowledgment path is the control (ADR-0004 decision 3).
The reviewer opens the filed PDF from Box side-by-side instead. If EVERY document loses
previews for days, note it for Seth (an OS/pyobjc update may have broken the bridge —
code).

---

## Symptom 10 — transient errors / a row stuck "claimed" (`estimate_transient`, `estimate_pending_fetch_failed`)

### Symptom
ERROR rows citing `estimate_transient` or `estimate_pending_fetch_failed`; the
ITS_Daemon_Health row DEGRADED; the same estimate re-serves for several cycles.

### What it means
Smartsheet / Box / Worker transport hiccups. Claimed rows re-serve by design (crash
recovery) — every step before the final result post is idempotent, so a re-run is safe.

### The low-class Tier-2 action
Wait 2–3 cycles (≈6 min). If one estimate loops for more than an hour with the same
error, capture the ITS_Errors lines (Correlation ID column) and escalate with them.
Never hand-edit D1 or the state files other than the documented flag-entry delete.
